watched.review: i love that you can definitely feel that the stories were made up after seeing the locations and i think that’s a very whimsical notion to think about imbuing fictional meaning on real places
watched.overview: A young poet drops his girlfriend off at her parents' house and is amazed by its size. He bumps into her father, meets her mother and sister, and they all end up spending a long day together; fueled by conversation, food and libations.

watched.name: My Dinner with Andre
watched.review: bro actually got one shotted by the beehive
watched.overview: Two old friends meet for dinner; as one tells anecdotes detailing his experiences, the other notices their differing worldviews.

watched.name: Passion
watched.review: no one is who you want them to be; a movie about honesty, love, violencethe cinematography was good but not great imo but the real star was the character work—the pacing was slow to start but crazy to see he was swinging like this so early in his filmography 4.75/5
watched.overview: A young couple's announcement of their engagement to their friends is followed by a series of interactions and conflicts that forces everyone to reflect on their lives.

watched.name: Y Tu Mamá También
watched.review: the narration carried but def a wild ride

watched.name: Anora
watched.overview: A young sex worker from Brooklyn gets her chance at a Cinderella story when she meets and impulsively marries the son of an oligarch. Once the news reaches Russia, her fairytale is threatened as his parents set out to get the marriage annulled.

watched.name: We Live in Time
watched.review: i enjoyed da lighting3.9/5
watched.overview: An up-and-coming chef and a recent divorcée find their lives forever changed when a chance encounter brings them together, in a decade-spanning, deeply moving romance.

watched.name: People We Meet on Vacation
watched.overview: Poppy's a free spirit. Alex loves a plan. After years of summer vacations, these polar-opposite pals wonder if they could be a perfect romantic match.

watched.name: Winter Light
watched.review: religious trauma final boss 😭
watched.overview: A Swedish pastor fails a loving woman, a suicidal fisherman and God.

watched.name: House
watched.review: utterly singulara good movie without a good narrative
watched.overview: Hoping to find a sense of connection to her late mother, Gorgeous takes a trip with her friends to visit her aunt's ancestral house in the countryside. The girls soon discover that there is more to the old house than meets the eye.

watched.name: Architecton
watched.review: like watching paint dry, but with striking visuals and a haunting score
watched.overview: An extraordinary journey through the material that makes up our habitat: concrete and its ancestor, stone. Victor Kossakovsky raises a fundamental question: how do we inhabit the world of tomorrow?

watched.name: Fantastic Planet
watched.overview: On the planet Ygam, the Draags, extremely technologically and spiritually advanced blue humanoids, consider the tiny Oms, human beings descendants of Terra's inhabitants, as ignorant animals. Those who live in slavery are treated as simple pets and used to entertain Draag children; those who live hidden in the hostile wilderness of the planet are periodically hunted and ruthlessly slaughtered as if they were vermin.

watched.name: I'm Your Man
watched.overview: Alma is a scientist coerced into participating in an extraordinary study in order to obtain research funds for her work. For three weeks, she has to live with a humanoid robot tailored to her character and needs, whose artificial intelligence is designed to be the perfect life partner for her. Enter Tom, a machine in human form in a class of its own, created solely to make her happy.

watched.name: An Autumn Afternoon
watched.review: a story about children growing up and leaving the nest i can see why ozu is so influential, but i can’t help but feel like yang does it better (e.g. yi yi) because of how the characters have interiority and the dialogue is more interestingi think that ultimately this film, whether intentionally or not, is about the patriarchy which makes for an interesting watching experience, at least on the conceptual level i liked the cinematography but i personally could not stand how dialogue starts and ends within the same shot which could’ve just been a technical thing but it felt very noticeable overall i feel like this just didn’t connect much with me but i do respect it for what it is
watched.overview: Widower Shuhei Hirayama's caretaker is his 24-year-old daughter, Michiko. Gradually, he comes to realize that Michiko should not be obliged to look after him for the rest of his life, so he arranges a marriage for her.

watched.name: The Rip
watched.overview: Trust frays when a team of Miami cops discovers millions in cash inside a run-down stash house, calling everyone — and everything — into question.

watched.name: Ip Man
watched.overview: A semi-biographical account of Yip Man, the first martial arts master to teach the Chinese martial art of Wing Chun. The film focuses on events surrounding Ip that took place in the city of Foshan between the 1930s to 1940s during the Second Sino-Japanese War. Directed by Wilson Yip, the film stars Donnie Yen in the lead role, and features fight choreography by Sammo Hung.

watched.name: Training Day
watched.overview: On his first day on the job as a narcotics officer, a rookie cop works with a rogue detective who isn't what he appears.

watched.name: The Theory of Everything
watched.overview: The Theory of Everything is the extraordinary story of one of the world’s greatest living minds, the renowned astrophysicist Stephen Hawking, who falls deeply in love with fellow Cambridge student Jane Wilde.

watched.name: Sentimental Value
watched.review: i never know where trier is gonna take us but i put my trust in him!!
watched.overview: Sisters Nora and Agnes reunite with their estranged father, the charismatic Gustav, a once-renowned director who offers stage actress Nora a role in what he hopes will be his comeback film. When Nora turns it down, she soon discovers he has given her part to an eager young Hollywood star.

watched.name: The Devil, Probably
watched.review: i love that bresson is interested in shots and framing beyond just faces i think this movie lives and dies by how much you are able to engage with the protagonist’s worldview; some might find it juvenile while others might see camus’ ultimate problem
watched.overview: Charles drifts through politics, religion and psychoanalysis, rejecting them all. Once he realises the depth of his disgust with the moral and physical decline of the society he lives in, he decides that suicide is the only option...

watched.name: Happyend
watched.review: a movie about youth and the times
watched.overview: In a near-future Japanese city bracing for a devastating earthquake, a group of teenage friends navigate personal struggles and fractured bonds amid rising tension.

watched.name: The Conversation
watched.review: rich in theme but deficient in action
watched.overview: A paranoid, secretive surveillance expert has a crisis of conscience when he suspects that the couple he is spying on will be murdered.

watched.name: On the Beach at Night Alone
watched.overview: In a foreign city, an actress wanders through a seaside town, reflecting on her relationship with a married man. How important is love in one’s life? Younghee wants to find out.

watched.name: Us and Them
watched.overview: Ten years ago, on a train home during the busy Spring Festival travel period, fate brings Xiaoxiao and Jianqing together. Like many young couples, they meet, fall in love, and strive to make it work, but eventually, the harsh realities of life make them drift apart. Ten years later, they run into each other again. Will they make the most of this second chance and rekindle what they once lost?

watched.name: Force Majeure
watched.review: a less morally complex but more socially engaged the drama
watched.overview: While holidaying in the French Alps, a Swedish family deals with acts of cowardliness as an avalanche breaks out.

watched.name: A Useful Ghost
watched.review: legiterally nothing i would change about this movie queerness, family, love, trauma, dreams, memory, grief, capitalism, progress, exploitation, extraction, forgiveness, forgetting, bearing witness, protest, revenge
watched.overview: Worried about her husband being allergic to dust, Nat, a recently-dead woman, returns as a ghost possessing a vacuum cleaner to clean the house and protect her family from other vengeful ghosts in the house. To become a useful ghost, she needs to get rid of the useless ones.

watched.name: Urchin
watched.review: a very promising debut!!
watched.overview: Mike, a rough sleeper in London, is trapped in a cycle of self-destruction as he attempts to turn his life around. Along the way, he encounters unexpected chances for a fresh start.

watched.name: Berlin Horse
watched.overview: Two fragments of 8mm home-movie footage shot by the artist near Berlin weave together in repeating cycles of action, temporal manipulation, and colour distortion, heightening the viewer’s awareness of film-time and the film-image, and perception of colour in motion.

watched.name: Us in the End
watched.review: ego is the enemy & you must be able to live with all your actions
watched.overview: Cisco and Cheska, a long-term couple, embark on a final "break-up trip" to decide whether to let go or fight for their relationship after years of growing apart due to conflicting ambitions.

watched.name: World of Glory
watched.review: love when andersson follows one person throughout the vignettes
watched.overview: The narrative portrays a plain man who guides the viewer through his life in a bleakly stylised world.

watched.name: Triangle of Sadness
watched.review: a bit on the nose sometimes but rlly enjoyed the social dynamics and character interactions
watched.overview: A celebrity model couple are invited on a luxury cruise for the uber-rich, helmed by an unhinged, alcoholic captain. What first appears Instagrammable ends catastrophically, leaving the survivors stranded on a desert island in a struggle of hierarchy.
